skill.name: meeting-insights-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes meeting transcripts to identify communication patterns, facilitation habits, and actionable improvement opportunities.
skill.summary: Reviews meeting transcripts to identify communication patterns, facilitation habits, and practical improvement opportunities.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are markdown formatting and ordinary communication-analysis prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration instructions were found.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown examples and inline code formatting. They do not define executable code, shell commands, or command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown fences, prompts, or report examples. I found no runnable Ruby, shell backticks, or user-input command execution path.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are keyword false positives from meeting-analysis text. The skill discusses communication, discovery of transcript files, and example outputs without cryptographic or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive prose about meetings and analysis goals. No weak cryptographic algorithm, host enumeration, or network scanning instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Meeting transcripts contain useful signals that are hard to review manually. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code identify patterns, examples, and focused next steps.
actual_capabilities: Reviews transcript files for communication patterns and recurring behaviors.
Identifies examples of conflict avoidance, interruptions, filler words, and listening signals.
Summarizes speaking ratios, question patterns, and facilitation habits when data supports them.
Compares multiple meetings or time periods to show changes in behavior.
Produces actionable recommendations with transcript-based examples.
limitations: Requires readable transcripts with enough speaker context for reliable analysis.
Cannot verify tone, intent, or body language from text alone.
Results depend on transcript quality, speaker labels, and timestamp accuracy.
Does not replace professional coaching, legal advice, or HR review.

use_cases.title: Review Leadership Presence
use_cases.description: Analyze recurring facilitation habits, directness, and follow-through across leadership meetings.

use_cases.title: Prepare Development Feedback
use_cases.description: Find concrete examples of communication strengths and growth areas for a review or coaching conversation.

use_cases.title: Track Communication Progress
use_cases.description: Compare transcript sets over time to measure changes in listening, interruptions, and clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Meeting
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this meeting transcript for my communication patterns. Focus on clarity, listening, and moments where I could have been more direct.

prompt_templates.title: Find Specific Behaviors
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these transcripts and identify times when I interrupted, used filler words, or avoided direct feedback. Include timestamps when available.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Meeting Sets
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the meetings in the Q1 and Q2 folders. Show how my facilitation style changed, including speaking ratio and question quality.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Coaching Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze all available transcripts, identify my top three communication patterns, and create a focused four-week improvement plan with measurable behaviors.

output_examples.input: Analyze my last five team meetings for facilitation patterns.
output_examples.output: A summary of recurring facilitation habits, speaking balance, inclusion of quieter participants, and three transcript-backed recommendations.

output_examples.input: Find moments where I avoided conflict in these transcripts.
output_examples.output: A list of indirect responses, missed opportunities to name issues, and alternative phrasing for each supported example.

output_examples.input: Compare my meetings from March and June.
output_examples.output: A trend report showing changes in interruptions, clarifying questions, filler word frequency, and listening indicators.

best_practices: Use transcripts with speaker labels, timestamps, and clear meeting dates.
Ask for one behavior or theme at a time when you need deep feedback.
Keep sensitive meeting data local and remove content that is not needed.
anti_patterns: Do not use poor transcripts as evidence for performance decisions without review.
Do not ask for conclusions about intent when the transcript only shows words.
Do not combine unrelated meeting types when you need precise trend analysis.

faq.question: What files work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Plain text, markdown, VTT, SRT, and other readable transcript formats work best when they include speaker labels.

faq.question: Can it analyze meeting recordings directly?
faq.answer: The skill is designed around transcripts. Recordings should be transcribed before analysis.

faq.question: Does it send meeting data to a third party?
faq.answer: No external transfer is described in the skill. Use your AI tool's normal privacy settings for uploaded or local files.

faq.question: Can it measure speaking time accurately?
faq.answer: It can estimate speaking ratios when transcripts include clear speaker turns and timestamps.

faq.question: Can it help with performance reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can find transcript-backed examples, but a human should review context before using them formally.

faq.question: Is this only for managers?
faq.answer: No. It can help managers, individual contributors, coaches, and anyone improving meeting communication.
